statement: Thornhaven is a small village of approximately 12 stone buildings around a central well.
All shutters in Thornhaven are closed and streets are empty at dusk.
The Broken Wheel is the only building showing light at dusk.
A crow watched the player from the inn sign with 'unsettling intelligence'.
notes: Ambiguous detail. Could be atmospheric flavor or a planted narrative hook (familiar, omen, spy). Do not invest resources without corroborating evidence. Flagged for monitoring.

statement: The innkeeper is a heavyset woman with grey-streaked hair and watchful eyes who did not offer her name.
The innkeeper said travelers don't come to Thornhaven 'this time of year', implying the player's arrival is unusual.
The innkeeper's grip on the bar cloth tightened when the player mentioned the missing scholar.
notes: Physical tell described by DM. Strong indicator that the innkeeper has relevant knowledge.

statement: The innkeeper denied knowledge of the scholar with: 'Scholars come and go. Can't keep track of every one.'
notes: The DM stated this directly. The verbal denial combined with the physical tell (ev-007) is notable.

statement: The innkeeper knows more about the missing scholar than she admitted.
notes: Derived from the combination of verbal denial and physical tell (tightened grip). Not confirmed — the innkeeper might simply be reacting to the sensitive topic generally.

statement: A young male villager with a bandaged arm made brief eye contact with the player when the missing scholar was mentioned, then looked away.
notes: Directly described by DM. The timing of the glance (during the scholar mention) is significant.

statement: The young villager's bandaged arm may be connected to an incident related to the missing scholar or the cause of village fear.
notes: Player hypothesis — not supported by direct evidence yet. Requires further investigation.
